items.title: Victorian regional train network shut down due to Telstra outage

children.content: A radio network fault thought to have been caused by the Telstra outage this morning has forced V/Line to suspend all regional train services across Victoria.

gallery.altText: A V/Line train stopped at a platform at Southern Cross station.

thumbnail.altText: A V/Line train stopped at a platform at Southern Cross station.

items.title: CFA leaders attempt to recall 'draft' submission to fatal summer fire inquiry

children.content: An administrative error during a parliamentary inquiry has led the CFA to inadvertently reveal exactly how short-staffed its stations were during last summer's deadly fire season.

image.altText: A CFA truck with a handpainted sign reading "Go Fight Your Own Fires Jacinta Allan"

items.title: Toddler waits seven hours for stitches during patient surge

children.content: A Central Victorian mother says she waited seven hours at Bendigo Health for her toddler to be treated for a head wound.

image.altText: A little girl sitting in an ambulance bed with a head wound.

items.title: Teammates of Melbourne footballer will sit next match out after death

children.content: Epping football club's reserves players have decided to skip this weekend's game following the death of their beloved teammate Nathan Fitzgerald on Monday night.

articles.title: 'My role model': Younger brother speaks after death of Melbourne footballer

children.content: The younger brother of footy player and teacher Nathan Fitzgerald, who died last night, has described him as a "gentle, wonderful" soul who cherished his family.

image.altText: Two men, one wearing a padded jacket and the other wearing a football guernsey, stand with heads bowed in contemplation

gallery.altText: Epping Football Netball Club president Luke Di Vincentis stands near the boundary fence

thumbnail.altText: Epping Football Netball Club president Luke Di Vincentis stands near the boundary fence

items.title: Embattled MP Moira Deeming apologises to supporters in social media video

children.content: The Liberal MP thanked her supporters and apologised for "distractions" in a video posted to her Facebook page, as questions still loom over her political future.

image.altText: Moira Deeming

items.title: Alleged Pam the Bird graffiti vandal faces court after bridge stand-off

children.content: A man has faced court after spending hours at the top of a 140-metre-high tower on Melbourne's Bolte Bridge, where he allegedly spray-painted the notorious graffiti symbol.

image.altText: A man dressed in a black top and pants walks along the rocks protruding from a river with his hands in the air.

items.title: Retail strip caught up in arson attack on inner Melbourne tobacco shop

children.content: More than 80 firefighters were called to Richmond where multiple businesses were alight after a shop was rammed this morning.

gallery.altText: A police officer walks down the middle of a city street where a fire truck is parked and flames can be seen coming from a shop.
Firefighters wearing helmets stand on a smoke filled street between a police car and fire engine with water coming from hoses.
A person walks through thick smoke over a city road talking into a walkie talkie near a fire truck and firefighters at night.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer walks down the middle of a city street where a fire truck is parked and flames can be seen coming from a shop.
Firefighters wearing helmets stand on a smoke filled street between a police car and fire engine with water coming from hoses.
A person walks through thick smoke over a city road talking into a walkie talkie near a fire truck and firefighters at night.

items.title: Melbourne to put on extravaganza rivalling Bollywood to welcome PM Modi

children.content: On a grey July day, a Melbourne community hall is bursting with colour as Australia's Indian community gears up to welcome Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

image.altText: Smiling women in red blouses, cream sarees, large bindis, hair tied, have hand behind hair as they sing.

items.title: Victorian town finally gets drinking water, but not everyone is happy

children.content: Victoria's largest town without drinking water finally gets a new potable supply, but it has left the community that has relied on tank water divided.

image.altText: Aerial shot of Kaniva.

items.title: Mushroom murder trial judge shares story in podcast

children.content: Retired judge Christopher Beale has reflected on his long legal career, his temporary pivot to becoming a Jesuit priest and the role he played in the Erin Patterson murder trial.

image.altText: A judge wearing traditional court garb speaks into a microphone.

items.title: AFP confirms threat made against Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi

children.content: Australian Federal Police says a young person was identified and issued a formal warning over an online threat made towards Mr Modi, ahead of his official visit later this week.

image.altText: Man wearing suit shakes hands with a second man, standing in front of a wall with two different flags on it.

items.title: Winmar statue removed during NAIDOC Week after assault conviction

children.content: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

gallery.altText: Bronze statue of Nicky Winmar at Perth Stadium.

thumbnail.altText: Bronze statue of Nicky Winmar at Perth Stadium.

items.title: 'Pretty rare to see clearance rates this low': Property slump tipped to continue

children.content: Less than half of all properties that went to auction in Australia were actually sold over the last week.

image.altText: A house with a sold sign in the front yard.

items.title: Man critical after car fire at Scoresby in Melbourne's east

children.content: Police are investigating a car fire that left a man with life-threatening burns in Melbourne's east.

image.altText: A white car that says "paramedic" is parked across a narrow road where police cars are parked at night.

items.title: Earthquake rocks community along Victoria's Great Ocean Road

children.content: The magnitude-3.8 earthquake struck at a depth of 10 kilometres along Victoria's popular Great Ocean Road.

image.altText: A map showing an earthquake location

items.title: Man's body pulled from Murray River at Barooga

children.content: NSW Police are investigating the death of a man who was found in the Murray River.

image.altText: Images of blue and white checkered police tape stretched across a suburban street.

items.title: When Jackie Chan came to Falls Creek and got hypothermia in a frozen lake

children.content: Thirty years ago a Hollywood crew turned Falls Creek into Ukraine, and Jackie Chan nearly froze getting the shot.

gallery.altText: Jackie chan in snow outfit and seal beanie smile and stands behind a video camera.

thumbnail.altText: Jackie chan in snow outfit and seal beanie smile and stands behind a video camera.

items.title: Broken back prompts apprentice electrician to chase footy career

children.content: Rather than ending his promising sporting prospects, a fractured back has prompted Mount Gambier tall Jobe Janeway to chase his dream of playing in the AFL.

image.altText: Jobe Janeway runs out with the South Australian team for a game against the Allies in the national championships

items.title: Fran Lebowitz — witty, withering and unfiltered

children.content: No-one emerges unscathed from Fran Lebowitz's quips, bon mots and scathing take-downs of New York's peculiarities and poor etiquette.

image.altText: Close up shot of Fran Lebowitz staring down the lens, on stage against a blue curtain wearing a navy jacket and white shirt

items.title: Search continues for woman missing in Melbourne's south

children.content: Police found a car belonging to Yun, also known as Michelle, in a car park on Friday but no other signs of her whereabouts.

image.altText: A CCTV image of a dark haired woman wearing a black top and black pants walks between a petrol bowser and a white car.

items.title: Investors 'all but gone' from Melbourne property market after budget

children.content: With clearance rates in Melbourne sitting just above 50 per cent, experts say sluggish sales show investors have lost trust in bricks and mortar.

image.altText: A man jogs past a brown brick house that has a large sign attached to the fence indicating it is for sale.

items.title: 'A lot to look forward to': Fans pay tribute to Socceroos despite World Cup elimination

children.content: Football fans across the country ride the gamut of emotions as the Socceroos suffer heartbreaking defeat in the FIFA World Cup, having come from behind to force extra time and penalties against Egypt in their knockout clash in Texas.

image.altText: Lucas Herrington looks disappointed

articles.title: Ange Postecoglou appointed head coach at Saudi club Al-Nassr

children.content: The 60-year-old takes over the Riyadh-based side led by Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo.

image.altText: A man looks out onto a football field.

items.title: 15yo honoured at memorial as third teenager charged with his murder

children.content: Darweish Mohamed, who was allegedly stabbed to death outside a Melbourne hospital on Wednesday, has been laid to rest as family and friends remembered him at a memorial today.

image.altText: Young man looks at camera in diagonal shot

items.title: Arson chemist investigates fatal house fire in Melbourne's south-east

children.content: A man believed to be in his 60s died after being pulled from a room engulfed in flames in a Springvale South home overnight.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: 175 years ago, Victoria struck gold. 230,000 scars remain

children.content: It has been 175 years since gold was first discovered in Victoria, and now a state government mapping project has discovered that there are far more relics of the gold rush left in the landscape than official records show.

gallery.altText: A person in high vis looks at a tablet next to a hole covered by a grate
A black and white photograph of children sitting on a mullock heap on the outskirts of town
Louise kneels on the ground in the bush, wearing high vis

thumbnail.altText: A person in high vis looks at a tablet next to a hole covered by a grate
A black and white photograph of children sitting on a mullock heap on the outskirts of town
Louise kneels on the ground in the bush, wearing high vis
